programme.title: No Man's Land
programme.desc: When a vigilante border patrol turns fatal, a man flees on horseback to Mexico, seeking forgiveness from the victim's father.

programme.title: Rage
programme.desc: Following the kidnapping and murder of his daughter (Aubrey Peeples), a reformed criminal (Nicolas Cage) returns to his old ways to exact vengeance.

programme.title: The Postcard Killings
programme.desc: A New York detective investigates the death of his daughter, who was murdered while on her honeymoon in London, the victim of an apparent serial killer.

programme.title: Dog Eat Dog
programme.desc: Three ex-cons (Nicolas Cage, Willem Dafoe, Christopher Matthew Cook) find themselves on the run from the mob and the police after botching a plan to kidnap a baby for a large ransom.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.title: Section 8
programme.desc: Sentenced to prison for avenging the murder of his family, a former soldier gets a second chance when a shadowy government agency recruits him for an assignment. However, he soon realizes that the very people who freed him are not what they seem.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: A Score to Settle
programme.desc: Diagnosed with a fatal condition, a mob enforcer leaves prison after 19 years and plots a bloody course of revenge against his old gang.

programme.title: True History of the Kelly Gang
programme.desc: An exploration of Australian bushranger Ned Kelly and his gang as they attempt to evade authorities during the 1870s.

programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: Life on Earth
programme.desc: Fish occur in populations of billions and there are over 30,000 species, more than in any other group of backboned animals. The development of the backbone was a crucial advance in evolution - and it probably came from a most unlikely source, a little jelly-like creature called a sea squirt.

programme.title: Voyages of Discovery
programme.desc: Explorer Paul Rose tells the story of his hero Fridjtof Nansen who, in 1892, announced a daring plan to be first to the North Pole, an idea considered so off-the-wall that no scientist would volunteer to join him on a venture they believed was nothing short of suicide. He allowed his ship to become stuck in the crushing pack ice, hoping it would drift to the Pole, and then set off on foot across the frozen wastes. Nansen became the forefather of polar exploration, inventing practical techniques that today allow people to survive, travel and work in the most hostile and forbidding places on our planet.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: If you think Britain has recently been on the end of some of the worst floods and storms ever experienced, think again. So says solar scientist Dr Lucie Green, as she takes a journey back through our most turbulent and dramatic weather history. She finds an 18th-century storm surge that killed over a thousand people working in open Somerset fields, a hurricane that drowned a fifth of the British Navy and winters so bitter that the country came close to total shutdown. But she also explores how our reactions to killer storms and cruel winters helped forge a weather science that today allows us to predict - and protect ourselves from - the worst extremes.

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham introduces a classic documentary from the BBC's archive, which takes a look at the worst winter of the 20th century in 1963. He also explores what we now know about how this big freeze affected Britain's wildlife, and how it would cope if we experienced another equally bad winter.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A specially curated night of musical gems, from the Royal Albert Hall. Alongside Beethoven's famous symphony are Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Strauss' Don Juan. Presented by Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann and featuring performances by the BBC Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Marin Alsop and Elim Chan.

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: The A303 is the road that passes Stonehenge on the way to the beaches of Devon and Cornwall. On the way, it whisks drivers through 5,000 years of remarkable moments in British history. And it is the star of this film made for armchair travellers and history lovers. Writer Tom Fort drives its 92-mile length in a lovingly restored Morris Traveller. Along the way, he has many adventures - he digs up the 1960s master plan for the A303's dreams of superhighway status, and meets up with a Neolithic traveller who knew the road like the back of his hand. He also gets to know a section of the Roman 303, uncovers a medieval murder mystery and discovers what lies at the end of the Highway to the Sun.

programme.title: Hans Zimmer: Hollywood Rebel
programme.desc: The story of one of the most celebrated and sought-after movie composers, as told by the man himself and his closest collaborators. Across a glittering 40-year career, Hans has redefined the movie score, thrilling audiences, pioneering new techniques and introducing new generations to the drama of orchestral music. In this portrait, Hans reveals the musical secrets of his craft - how he goes about terrifying, moving and raising an audience's spirits through his music.

programme.title: BBC Proms
programme.desc: A specially curated night of musical gems, from the Royal Albert Hall. Alongside Beethoven's famous symphony are Mussorgsky's A Night on a Bare Mountain and Strauss' Don Juan. Presented by Petroc Trelawny and Georgia Mann and featuring performances by the BBC Symphony Orchestra, conducted by Marin Alsop and Elim Chan.

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: If you think Britain has recently been on the end of some of the worst floods and storms ever experienced, think again. So says solar scientist Dr Lucie Green, as she takes a journey back through our most turbulent and dramatic weather history. She finds an 18th-century storm surge that killed over a thousand people working in open Somerset fields, a hurricane that drowned a fifth of the British Navy and winters so bitter that the country came close to total shutdown. But she also explores how our reactions to killer storms and cruel winters helped forge a weather science that today allows us to predict - and protect ourselves from - the worst extremes.

programme.title: A303: Highway to the Sun
programme.desc: The A303 is the road that passes Stonehenge on the way to the beaches of Devon and Cornwall. On the way, it whisks drivers through 5,000 years of remarkable moments in British history. And it is the star of this film made for armchair travellers and history lovers. Writer Tom Fort drives its 92-mile length in a lovingly restored Morris Traveller. Along the way, he has many adventures - he digs up the 1960s master plan for the A303's dreams of superhighway status, and meets up with a Neolithic traveller who knew the road like the back of his hand. He also gets to know a section of the Roman 303, uncovers a medieval murder mystery and discovers what lies at the end of the Highway to the Sun.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: In the wild North Atlantic, massive whale pods, giant turtles and monstrous jellyfish ride the Gulf Stream, a huge ocean current that becomes a migration superhighway and helps warm northern Europe. Meanwhile, fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to reap the current's natural fertility.

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Greece
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke unpicks the reasons behind the dazzling revolution that gave birth to classical Greek art, asking how the Greeks got so good so quickly. He travels to the beautiful Valley of the Temples in Agrigento, and to the island of Mozia to see the astonishing charioteer found there in 1979, and marvels at the athletic bodies of the warriors dragged from the seabed - the Riace Bronzes. It was a creative explosion that covered architecture, sculpting in marble, casting in bronze, even painting on vases. Perhaps the most powerful factor was also its greatest legacy - a fascination with the naked human body.

programme.title: Private Life of a Masterpiece
programme.desc: Whistler's portrait of his elderly Puritan mother is one of the most satirised paintings of all time. Yet when his sombre masterpiece was first unveiled, it was seen as a radical departure from the sentimental images beloved of the Victorians, and which Whistler loathed. How did the portrait, born of Whistler's complex relationship with his mother, change the course of painting so drastically?

programme.title: One Hundred Great Paintings
programme.desc: Milton Brown presents a closer look at James Whistler's painting Nocturne in Black and Gold: The Falling Rocket at the Institute of Art, Detroit.

programme.title: Becoming Frida Kahlo
programme.desc: Biographer Martha Zamora describes Frida's rebellious schooldays, where she falls in with the boys and meets Diego Rivera, a superstar artist who will go on to change her life. But in 1925, a tragic accident thwarts Frida's ambition of becoming a doctor. Left facing a life of pain and injury, she channels this pain and uses it as the catalyst for her artistic career.

programme.title: Stolen: Catching the Art Thieves
programme.desc: In 1994, Munch’s The Scream is ripped from a gallery wall in Oslo. The detectives leading the investigation describe a complex and dangerous investigation which exposes the lengths to which criminals will go to gain a get-out-of-jail card for murder.

programme.title: Atlantic: The Wildest Ocean on Earth
programme.desc: In the wild North Atlantic, massive whale pods, giant turtles and monstrous jellyfish ride the Gulf Stream, a huge ocean current that becomes a migration superhighway and helps warm northern Europe. Meanwhile, fishermen battle for survival in mountainous seas as they try to reap the current's natural fertility.

He travels to the beautiful Valley of the Temples in Agrigento, and to the island of Mozia to see the astonishing charioteer found there in 1979, and marvels at the athletic bodies of the warriors dragged from the seabed - the Riace Bronzes. programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: At the home of Scottish football, Glasgow’s Hampden Park stadium, Michael admires the oldest football trophy in the world and hears how the 'passing game' was born there. From Scotland’s busiest railway station, the splendid Glasgow Central, Michael heads to the M8 motorway to see 16 lanes of traffic slicing through the city centre. At the Kelvingrove Art Gallery, Michael hears how, after the Second World War, artist Joan Eardley set up a studio amid the tenements of Townhead and painted children from Glasgow’s slums. Michael finishes this leg of the journey in the planned, modernist new town of Cumbernauld, where he hears about the utopian ideals that shaped its design and architecture.

programme.title: Walking With...
programme.desc: Broadcaster Kate Garraway goes for an invigorating walk along the edge of the Cotswold Hills. As she passes through the patch where Kate first cut her teeth as a reporter, she takes time to appreciate the stunning landscapes. From the panoramic viewpoint of Painswick Beacon, Kate descends through beech woods to the village of Upton St Leonards, soaking up the sounds of nature as she goes. From there she climbs Cooper’s Hill and wanders down to the church at Great Witcombe. Kate relishes the chance to have nothing to do but take in the 'joy and rhythm of the countryside' as she crosses the gentle, rolling hills with just a 360-degree camera for company. Along the way, she meets local craftspeople who are inspired by the ‘living theatre’ of the landscapes. Kate finds her natural surroundings a source of inspiration and resilience as she reflects on a year of challenges and change.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth realises that Richard is bored after taking early retirement and provides him with a hobby - being artistic with a video camera. However, it's a decision she will live to regret.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: Victor and Margaret head off for a weekend at the seaside. Victor is presented with a most unusual and unwelcome gift.

programme.title: She-Wolves: England's Early Queens
programme.desc: In the medieval and Tudor world there was no question in people's minds about the order of God's creation - men ruled and women didn't. A king was a warrior who literally fought to win power then battled to keep it. Yet despite everything that stood in their way, a handful of extraordinary women did attempt to rule medieval and Tudor England. In this series, historian Dr Helen Castor explores seven queens who challenged male power, the fierce reactions they provoked and whether the term 'she wolves' was deserved. Eight hundred years ago, Matilda came within a hair's breadth of being the first woman to be crowned queen of England in her own right. Castor explores how Matilda reached this point and why her bid for the throne ultimately failed. Her daughter-in-law Eleanor of Aquitaine was an equally formidable woman. Despite being remembered as the queen of courtly love, in reality during her long life she divorced one king and married another, only to lead a rebellion against him. She only finally achieved the power she craved in her seventies.

programme.title: Castles: Britain's Fortified History
programme.desc: Sam Willis looks at the history of the castle from its first appearance with the Normans in 1066 to the longest siege on English soil at Kenilworth Castle 200 years later. The castle arrived as an instrument of invasion but soon became a weapon with which unruly barons challenged the Crown. Tintagel Castle, the place where King Arthur is said to have been conceived, is also on the itinerary. It remains one of the most evocative of castles to this day, drawing visitors from around the world with its tales of myth and legend.

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: Why did the entire world stand by in 1994 as nearly one million people were murdered over one hundred days of incomprehensible terror in Rwanda? American diplomats describe events on the ground and in Washington as this tragedy unfolded. What did the White House know, when did they know it and why didn’t they act? The horrendous results of ignoring the genocide in Rwanda haunts the Washington decision-makers to this day. President Clinton refers to it as 'the greatest failure of my life'.

programme.title: Corridors of Power: Should America Police the World?
programme.desc: Three years after the civil war in Bosnia, the world looked like a very different place. The Bosnian genocide had been stopped, but Muslim separatists in the Kosovo region rekindled old conflicts that led President Clinton’s White House to consider Serbian President Milošević’s regime a threat to their own civilians. With post-Soviet Russia in crisis, Boris Yeltsin, the Russian president, criticised at home for being 'an American puppet', strongly opposed any western intervention against their old allies in the Balkans. Nato did intervene, but at what cost? Boris Yeltsin is forced to resign, leaving a young and promising KGB officer named Vladimir Putin to take his place.

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: Global Eye returns for a new series, bringing you forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye - the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit - and featuring expert global correspondents taking you to the heart of the biggest international issues. This week, Shawn Yuan is in Mexico investigating the extraordinary story of one of the world's most wanted fugitives - a man accused of fuelling the global fentanyl crisis. And from Mexico to Kenya, BBC Eye goes inside the online manosphere.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Ammie Sekhon heads behind the scenes of WWE to see the tech that takes the show around the world. Shiona McCallum explores the latest sleep analysing technology. We meet the professor using biomechanics to help women find the best bra and tackle breast pain. And Andrew Rogers tests out the AI coming to the dentist's chair.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI can write, code, plan and problem-solve in seconds. But as we hand more tasks over to machines, what happens to our own skills? In this episode, AI Decoded investigates whether AI is enhancing human intelligence or quietly eroding it. From doctors and software engineers to students growing up with AI tutors, we explore new research suggesting that reliance on AI could affect critical thinking, expertise and decision-making. Joining Marc are leading paediatric neurosurgeon Owase Jeelani, digital transformation researcher Dr Tapani Rinta-Kahila and AI Decoded co-host Dr Stephanie Hare.

programme.title: China's Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Traditional Chinese gardens represent every aspect of the natural landscape. They are an inherent part of people's lives, both physically and spiritually. Alastair Sooke travels from north to south, to examine ancient paintings and visits surviving historic gardens. His aim is to understand how Chinese aesthetics and philosophy are reflected in the design of traditional gardens.

programme.title: The Media Show
programme.desc: The Trump administration has issued subpoenas to several New York Times journalists following reporting about alleged security concerns involving President Trump's new Air Force One, a plane donated by Qatar. The case has prompted debate over the balance between protecting national security and safeguarding press freedom. Claire Atkinson, founder of The Media Mix newsletter, outlines the latest developments. The BBC's latest annual report comes as the corporation warns that public service media is facing a period of jeopardy. The figures show a further fall in licence fee-paying households, alongside changing audience habits and continued financial pressures as charter renewal negotiations continue. Rhodri Talfan Davies, the BBC's deputy director-general, sets out the corporation's position. Broadcaster and author Iain Dale reflects on a career spanning politics, publishing, blogging and radio following the publication of his memoir Have I Said Too Much?. The interview covers the enduring appeal of the radio phone-in, the influence of social media on broadcasting, and changing relationships between politicians and traditional media.

programme.title: World's Ultimate Adventures
programme.desc: Cultural historian Janina Ramirez has long been fascinated by the legend of Troy, immortalised in the epic works of Homer. The tales of a ten-year battle, the beauty of Queen Helen and the trick of the Trojan horse that brought an ancient civilisation to its knees has inspired storytellers from Shakespeare to Christopher Nolan, from Chaucer to Margaret Atwood. Up until 150 years ago, many believed that the ancient city was a mythological place, but on this journey, Janina travels to north western Turkey to explore its ancient ruins. In the Museum of Troy, the glitter of gold will catch her eye as she gets up close with some of treasures that have been unearthed from the site. She crosses the glistening Aegean Sea to the island of Bozcaada, where she'll learn how to hunt the waters for a local seafood delicacy before making the journey north to the Gallipoli Peninsula. Here, she'll walk the beaches where Allied troops landed in 1915 and explore the World War I trenches that remain. Janina is astounded by the beauty of the region, and it is the layers of buried human history that will stay with her.

programme.title: Prayer and Reflection
programme.desc: Join us for the peaceful and spiritually uplifting Isha prayers from the Belfast Islamic Centre, which serves as a hub for Muslims across Northern Ireland. The prayers are the final of the five daily prayers in Islam, and take place after sunset, providing an opportunity for worshippers to reflect on their day and look ahead to the future.

programme.title: The Addams Family 2
programme.desc: Wednesday is delighted to find a like-minded genius, who happens to be the sponsor of her school's science fair. When an irritating lawyer arrives at the family home, Gomez takes the Addams on an impromptu and diverting trip around America, but Wednesday is haunted by doubt. Why is she so different from the rest of her family?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Join Christina Trevanion for today’s show as she heads back to school to learn about life for Victorian children. Two red and blue teams are hoping to go to the top of the class with the help of experts Kate Bliss and Richard Madley.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones is in the historic city of Hull to explore how music, culture and faith are bringing new life to this historic city, resulting in it being named one of the top places in the world to visit in 2026. Aled goes to Hull Minster and learns from musical director Mark Keith how its choir was saved ten years ago and is now thriving, partly due to an innovative partnership that takes music into local primary schools to spread the joy of singing. Aled also visits the workshop of Helen Richardson, who transforms old or discarded items into treasure, all inspired by God’s restorative powers. And Aled joins a remarkable local church that meets in its own adventure park, complete with high rope courses and zip wires! He hears from Minister Jarrod Cooper how the facilities help promote fun, family and a sense of community. With glorious music, including some specially recorded hymns from a packed Hull Minster: 1. Hark, My Soul, It Is yhe Lord – Hull Minster 2. A Gaelic Blessing – Hull Minster 3. Holy Forever – Way Church, Wigan 4. O Thou Who Camest from Above – Hull Minster 5. King of Kings, Majesty – The Mint Methodist Church, Exeter 6. How Great Thou Art – Hull Minster

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley’s buyer today is chasing an inspiring landscape. She wants a new Carmarthenshire home that has as much land as her £450,000 budget can muster. She’s also keen to make some green-fingered, like-minded friends in the area, so a trip to meet some volunteers at the National Garden of Wales is also on the agenda.

programme.title: The Mating Game
programme.desc: The strategies used by animals to find and defend their mates in the grasslands of Namibia, the Serengeti and Australia. Here, mating strategies are on show for all to see.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Revealed as the web-slinging superhero, Peter Parker wants the world to forget, so he begs a spell from Dr Strange, but with grave consequences. Foes from across the multiverse materialise – along with an unexpected friend or two. Blockbuster Marvel action movie.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita continues her east coast road trip, visiting the Naze, a nature reserve on the Essex coast. Here, coastal erosion is particularly severe, with around 2 to 3 metres lost from the cliffs every year. But there are benefits to a crumbling coastline – it can reveal 55 million years of history. Anita joins a group of enthusiastic fossil hunters on the lookout for remnants of the coast's fascinating past. She also discovers what has been done to protect the area’s heritage and visits one of over a thousand brightly coloured beach huts overlooking the North Sea. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the hidden histories found across our landscape.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of Spain v Argentina, the 2026 FIFA World Cup final from the New Jersey Stadium, New York. After five weeks and 103 matches, just two teams remain in contention to win football’s biggest prize.

programme.title: BBC Weekend News

programme.title: The Full Monty
programme.desc: Blockbuster comedy about a group of unemployed Sheffield steelworkers who turn to stripping to try to escape the poverty trap into which they have fallen.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: The police K9 unit raid the home of an illegal pit bull puppy breeder, and officers are on the trail of a fake tour guide targeting tourists.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Rick Stein leaves the verdant coast and turns inland, travelling 400 kilometres west to the farming hub of Moree.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland’s County Down, Jules Hudson helps Siobhan, with support from her sister, search for the perfect Emerald Isle home for her and her husband after years of living in the West Midlands. With golf firmly on the agenda, properties include a stunning house on the shores of the iconic Strangford Lough. Jules also meets a barrister turned farmer and his wife, who swapped courtrooms for country life.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: After losing both parents, a pro-footballer’s daughter calls in JB and JJ to help turn heirlooms into cash for Una’s goal - a dream holiday with her beloved pooch, Patricia.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Gyles Brandreth. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Builder by day and big cat investigator by night, Gary Ridley is on a quest to solve one of Britain's enduring mysteries. Are big cats roaming freely in the countryside?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: It’s tense between Lauren and Mark, Vicki struggles in the wake of recent events, and Denise and Jack are feeling the pressure.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Criminal gangs are targeting the rental market to take over properties and use them to run their operations. Panorama reporter Hazel Martin speaks to flat owners who paid reputable agencies to do tenancy checks but still ended up handing over their keys to criminals using fake IDs and references. One flat was turned into a drugs factory, another became a brothel, and a third was sublet and ransacked. Even when there appears to be clear evidence of criminal behaviour, gangs are escaping prosecution, and landlords say it can take months to repossess their properties.

programme.title: Celebrity Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Choirmaster Gareth Malone is hoping that the New Forest property market hits all the right notes as he enlists the help of Sonali Shah to find him and his family a new home in the Hampshire countryside. With mum Sian along for assistance, Gareth has one big request: to find a property with enough space to fit a grand piano! A beautifully converted former schoolhouse may just provide the answer, but the endless possibilities and potential at a 1920s thatched cottage really strike a chord with the music maestro. Away from the property search, Gareth and Sonali take to the water on the promise of high-octane adventure and adrenaline on the south coast.

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: A revelation threatens to ruin not only Sally's legal challenge, but the entire bedrock of her family life. Meanwhile, DCI Morgan closes in on his prime suspect.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: I, Jack Wright
programme.desc: Sally faces the wrath of an increasingly unhinged Arnaud. Rose shocks Bobby by taking a huge risk, Emily uncovers a revelation, and DCI Morgan finally brings charges for the murder of Jack Wright.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Toby Jones explores an extraordinary acting dynasty on his mum’s side, and he is determined to discover whether his dad’s stories of a connection to India are fact or fiction.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. It's a milestone moment for the park's young Amur tigers as sisters Ginger Biscuit and Seeka begin preparations to leave home. As part of the international breeding programme, they'll soon move to new collections to help secure the future of one of the world's rarest big cats. But before they can go, the keepers face the challenge of preparing them for life apart and training them to enter their transport crates for the very first time. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate look back at an extraordinary conservation adventure when Longleat keepers travelled to Tanzania to help renowned conservationist Tony Fitzjohn relocate endangered African wild dogs. This remarkable story would go on to influence conservation work back at the park for years to come. Elsewhere, Megan introduces the park's emus to an unusual new treat, while the recently arrived maned wolves begin settling into their new home as keepers learn to tell the three sisters apart.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: Two men are lured to a spot in Cambridgeshire under the pretence of a business meeting. But they’re kidnapped, threatened with weapons by masked men and forced to transfer a large amount of the company’s money. Police need to unpick a complicated web of phone calls and CCTV to find their moment of proof. And a couple who become ill after eating curry are found to have been poisoned. With one person dead, police are hunting for a murderer.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: A fibre analyst uses a groundbreaking technique to solve the murder of 91-year-old Edwin Hiles, and a forensic fire investigator helps to uncover the truth behind a tragic house fire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An uncle is on hand to help his niece create a family home in Bristol. Dion’s in the dark in Leeds, but it’s nothing a lick of white paint won’t sort out. Jacqui’s in west London, where she’s hoping to get a free massage from the new owner of a ground floor flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Knock knock! Who’s there? It’s Danny Sebastian banging on about the history of door knockers, from Ancient Greece right through to the lion on the famous front door of 10 Downing Street. In Caroline Hawley’s saleroom, Danny sees an impressive collection of door knockers, amassed by one man over a lifetime, now going under the hammer. And Caroline is the right woman to be selling them – it turns out she’s a massive fan of door knockers too! Over at the antiques fair, which team is knocking on the door of a profit? Is it partners Rob and Sam, or married couple Janet and Bob? Expert Philip Serrell wonders whether his blues will sail away with a nautical themed jug, and Charles winds up his reds with a bird in a cage automaton – until they see the price tag. It ain’t ‘cheep’!

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A former British nurse hopes to survive tough police training to fulfil her dream of becoming a qualified constable, and an officer finally finds love.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Australia
programme.desc: Leaving Moree, Rick travels through the traditional lands of the Gomeroi people, where he meets First Nations’ pioneer Kerrie Saunders.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah is in the Peak District helping Andrew, and his faithful dog Edna, swap Amsterdam for a home in the UK’s first National Park. Supported by his big sister, and with a budget of £375,000, he’s hoping for a characterful cottage with spectacular scenery. Property picks include homes boasting fantastic views and a Mystery House which comes with something special that should appeal to his sibling! Whilst there, Sonali also discovers how local woodlands are being transformed, as young native trees are creating a greener future following the impact of ash dieback.

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Nigel Havers presides as members of the public with interesting items to sell come face to face with five dealers who bid against each other to try to bag themselves a bargain. Before facing the dealers, each seller has their item valued by auctioneer Simon Bower. Simon also gives them top tips on the history and key characteristics of their piece, so that when they enter the bidding room, they have all the information they need to try to haggle the best possible price from the dealers. All the dealers are spending their own money but have no idea what the sellers have brought in.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: In Scotland, Izzie and Robin help Anne declutter her crammed flat to raise funds for a campervan adventure. But can a dishevelled Hindu deity bring them the fortunes they need?

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Jack struggles to cope with his emotions, Lauren prepares to make a decision, and Joel struggles being back in Walford.

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: It’s week 2, and judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young put the 11 remaining sewers through their paces with three challenges themed around prints and colours. First, the sewers take on the Pattern Challenge, constructing a pattern-matched shell top with adaptive elements, including invisible side-zips and a high-low hem. In the Transformation Challenge, they are given second-hand clothes in purple and brown, the judges’ least-favourite colours, and have to create garments that will convince them to change their minds. Finally, in the Made to Measure, the sewers must create an eye-catching outfit in just one colour.

programme.title: Murder 24/7
programme.desc: When Jo is found stabbed at home, her husband is arrested. As forensic evidence and witness accounts are examined, detectives grow highly sceptical of his story.

programme.title: Harriet
programme.desc: The story of Harriet Tubman, chronicling her escape from slavery and transformation into a leading abolitionist fighter, who freed hundreds of slaves in the years before the American Civil War.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick regrets showing off to his new number two, Towerblock. Simon and Bird come to blows over a special book belonging to the lieutenant colonel.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: A blast from the past knocks Nick off his game. Bird tries to teach Mary a lesson that the padre is out of touch with the troops.

programme.title: Bluestone 42
programme.desc: Nick and the lieutenant colonel find themselves in an unexpected battle of wills. Rocket gets himself a pet rat, much to Bird's disgust.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animation. It’s 1995, and welcome to Dave’s Games: your one-stop shop for all things video games, from computers and consoles to poppers and Pogs - and how can you forget the floor-to-ceiling piles of plastic game cartridges? Self-appointed ‘gaming legend’, shop owner and father (in that order), Dave is the king of his own crumbling kingdom. Sure, there is mould on the ceiling and some of the oldest video games you’ve ever seen (even for 1995), but it’s the only games shop in town. At least until the gigantic, shiny, futuristic rival shop Turbo Gamez opens just down the road. And they also happen to be releasing the newest and most sophisticated piece of gaming technology the world has ever seen: the first PlayStation…

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: On a Bradford estate, Lenny and Mullet find an abandoned car and turn it into a DIY spaceship. A kitchen-sink science fiction comedy about estate childhoods, big dreams and escaping without going anywhere.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: The Zygons, a race of shapeshifting aliens, have been living in secret amongst us on Earth, unknown and unseen - until now! When Osgood is kidnapped by a rogue gang of Zygons, the Doctor, Clara and Unit must scatter across the world in a bid to set her free. But will they reach her in time, and can they stop an uprising before it is too late?

programme.title: The Notebook
programme.desc: In the run-up to the Second World War, a young couple's relationship falls foul of class prejudice.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Best friends Evie and Noa have watched films together since they first met at university. Eight years on, Evie isn’t coping with life anymore, so she’s moved back home to live with her mum, Suz, and sister Izzie in the Manchester suburbs. Film Club’s moved with Evie – to Suz’s garage. Unable to face the world outside and trapped at home with little else to occupy her, Evie pours all her creativity into decorating the space beautifully to reflect the movie they’re watching every week and making herself the perfect costume. But a change on the horizon is bringing up newfound feelings for Evie and Noa. Or feelings they’ve never admitted to themselves or each other…

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Noa attends a taster day for his new job in Bristol, Evie pulls out all the stops for Film Club – there’s a lot riding on tonight.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Evie’s still reeling from the message she saw on Noa’s phone. But when the two are left alone together, something extraordinary happens. Is it the start of a new journey for Evie?

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: While Evie throws herself headlong at life, Suz and Josh worry it’s too much, too soon. Meanwhile, Noa realises he has to face up to his feelings...

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Her world rocked, Evie has retreated to the safety of the garage. But with a new understanding, Suz and Izzie create a unique version of Film Club to bring back Evie’s smile.

programme.title: Film Club
programme.desc: Noa didn’t want a big goodbye, but now the day’s finally come for him to leave, will Evie let him go that easily? It’s not the only big decision she has to face...

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Stay-at-home-daughter Chi is suddenly forced to fend for herself when her parents die in a car crash. Chi learns she will only inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left in the parents' will, the first of which is, ironically, learn to drive. Meanwhile, Chi's highly strung auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her dependent pest of a niece, but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Chi ventures out into Ramsgate in search of a driving license and infiltrates the family of a mild-mannered driving instructor in the process.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Chi spectacularly fails a trial period at the pharmacy with Karen, but Dave kindly lets her shadow him on his delivery rounds. On a drop-off to a retirement home, Chi finds her tribe... old people. She also falls in love with Linda, an elderly lady who seems to think Chi is her husband. Elsewhere, Dave has a health scare, and Karen starts to think she might be the fertility issue.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Pancake day is a highly sentimental time for Chi. Dave confides in Lucas about his health scare. They vow to make the most of every moment, and that begins with pancake day.

programme.title: BBC New Comedy Awards
programme.desc: Five budding comedians perform, but only one can go through to the grand final of the BBC New Comedy Awards 2024.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
programme.desc: Despite an Avengers encounter, Peter Parker’s amazing abilities and Tony Stark-supplied costume are stuck serving only his own New York neighbourhood. Then an encounter with crooks using alien tech, led by the cynical Vulture, draws him into a dangerous adventure – almost as perilous as wanting to take the girl of his dreams to their high school homecoming dance.

programme.title: Spider-Man: Far from Home
programme.desc: Dodging responsibilities, Peter Parker takes a school trip to Europe but lands in monstrous trouble. Nick Fury expects Spider-Man to step up and help prevent further disaster, alongside a newly arrived hero, but the teen from Queens doubts himself.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine show with Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond. This week's show is an American special as Jeremy, Richard and James try to prove that a fly-drive holiday to Florida would be more fun if you bought a car instead of renting one. Having picked up some wheels for no more than 1000 dollars each, the boys embark on an epic road trip to find out who got the best deal, encountering heat, flies and very angry locals along the way.

programme.title: Eddie Hall: The Beast v The Mountain
programme.desc: Dubbed the heaviest boxing match in history, in March 2022, two of the strongest men in the world took to the ring in Dubai in what was a dramatic climax to one of the most bitter rivalries ever to hit the sporting world. With exclusive and intimate access to Eddie and his family in the months leading up to the fight and at the main event itself, this documentary follows the highs and lows, and all the laughs and tears, as Eddie balances a young family with a punishing and obsessively strict training regime, all the while battling to keep his mental health in check. Can the working-class man from Stoke restore his honour and achieve the retribution he so desperately desires against the towering Hollywood star?

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar investigates how a protest outside an asylum seeker hotel turned into a riot, uncovering a blueprint for a national wave of violence that eight months later would affect us all.

programme.title: Small Town, Big Riot
programme.desc: Mobeen Azhar returns to Kirkby to try to connect the dots between a whirlwind of misinformation and growing unrest outside sites housing asylum seekers. He soon finds out that a local man with white supremacist views and connections was campaigning outside the Suites hotel in the run-up to the riot. The question is, how much did this influence what took place days later? With that man now serving time in prison for stirring up racial hatred, Mobeen’s investigation takes him further afield to a former RAF base called Scampton. Since the night of the riot in Kirkby, the policy of the then Conservative government shifted. They began shutting down asylum seeker hotels in favour of bigger sites like Scampton.

programme.title: Doctor Who (2005–2022)
programme.desc: Shapeshifting Zygons are everywhere in the UK, and there is no way of knowing who to trust. With UNIT neutralised, only the Doctor stands in their way. But how do you stop a war? And what can the Doctor do to save his friends?
