programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Revealed as the web-slinging superhero, Peter Parker wants the world to forget, so he begs a spell from Dr Strange, but with grave consequences. Foes from across the multiverse materialise – along with an unexpected friend or two. Blockbuster Marvel action movie.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine. Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Spain, with Clarkson in a McLaren 12C Spider, Hammond driving a Ferrari 458 Spider and May using an Audi R8 V10 Spyder. Over the course of their travels the three presenters and their mid-range convertible supercars encounter mountain passes, ghost towns and an incredible abandoned airport, before building their own street circuit in the deserted developments of Madrid. Also in this show, Sherlock and Star Trek actor Benedict Cumberbatch is the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine show. Richard Hammond drives a Formula One car and Jeremy Clarkson and James May search for the first car with the control layout we know today. The Vauxhall VXR8 grunts around the test track, and there's a review of a BMW that can drive itself. James Blunt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and Lewis Hamilton is a special studio guest.

programme.title: Rush
programme.desc: The story of the rivalry between legendary F1 racing champions Niki Lauda and James Hunt, two charismatic drivers with entirely different approaches and personalities.

programme.title: Madonna at the BBC
programme.desc: A look back at a selection of magical Madonna moments on various BBC shows, from her first Top of the Pops appearance with Holiday in 1984 right up to the present day. This collection covers Madonna's journey from Material Girl to Queen of Pop and captures how over the years her ability to combine charisma, controversy and classic pop tunes has made her the female icon of her times and a true global superstar.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Ruth Madeley unearths a long-buried family secret about an adoption, and a mystery that requires DNA testing and meticulous genealogical detective work to uncover a lost ancestor.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood takes an alphabetical journey through the world of gardening, getting inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular garden presenters and programmes. In this episode, she's exploring subjects that all begin with the letter P. Programmes include: Gardeners' World, The One Show, RHS Chelsea Flower Show, Grow Your Own Veg and Flora Britannica. With presenters: Alys Fowler, Mike Dilger, Rachel de Thame, Christine Walkden, Monty Don, Laurence Llewellyn-Bowen and Carol Klein.

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Christine Walkden is in the air above two glorious Gloucestershire gardens. At Hidcote Manor Garden she cuts an underperforming shrub down to size and meets Sue, for whom the garden is an inspiration. At Kiftsgate Court Gardens she tackles an enormous rambling rose. We discover the arts and crafts heritage of the Cotswolds.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The next stop on Anita's road trip is West Mersea in Essex. While the county may be known for engineering and car manufacturing, along its coastline one industry has a much longer history. Oysters have been harvested here for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Over the decades, oyster farming has adapted to changing environmental conditions and the arrival of the non-native Pacific rock oyster. To experience what oyster harvesting is like today, Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters. She also meets the team investigating how the non-native Pacific rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around our coastline.

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek goes island hopping, visiting Caldey off the Pembrokeshire coast, then Anglesey, known for its seabirds, before finishing in south Wales at Barry Island, his childhood home.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: The weather in this year’s growing season is making gardening a real challenge at Beechgrove, with cold grey skies and periods of high temperature giving Ruth Vichos and Brian Cunningham plenty to deal with. Ruth is in her vertical garden, where she has strawberries, blossoms and various climbing varieties all demonstrating what you can produce if you are short of space, using stacking systems and plants that will follow vertical surfaces. Brian reviews the wildflower beds he sowed in April, which will become a great resource for pollinators and the rest of the garden’s ecosystem. Brian and Ruth look at the diseases and pests that might be attacking your garden favourites right now and what to do about them. There is a visit to a great garden in East Lothian, and all the week’s handy hints.

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt celebrates the best of seasonal produce with the help of some favourite Saturday Kitchen chefs. Bryn Williams puts courgettes centre stage by dressing them in a sharp and zesty citrus dressing and topping with a fillet of pan-fried sea bream. Meanwhile, Poppy O’Toole has three viral recipes – hot honey chicken, feta and pomegranate chopped salad and crispy smashed potato salad. Gennaro Contaldo tampers with an Italian classic, Ravneet Gill has a showstopping mango roulade with a creamy panna cotta filling, and Carmen O’Neal has a rundown of the best rums from around the world. Finally Vanessa Williams tucks into either her food heaven, butterflied bass with fries, or her food hell, pork and black bean tacos. Meanwhile, Rick Stein embarks on a moonlit fishing trip, and the Hairy Bikers hope to catch tuna in Sardinia.

programme.title: A Man for All Seasons
programme.desc: Multi-award-winning historical drama about Sir Thomas More's refusal to acknowledge Henry VIII's break with the Roman Catholic Church. King Henry VIII wants the pope to grant him a divorce from his barren wife to marry Anne Boleyn in order to get a male heir. Above all, he wants the endorsement of his newly appointed chancellor and widely respected scholar Sir Thomas More. But More has a divine conscience and will not consent to a break with the church of Rome. The scene is set for a struggle between the power of a king and a man who loves God more than his life. Paul Scofield's performance won one of the film's six Oscars, which also included the best picture award. Adapted by Robert Bolt from his own stage play.

programme.title: I Was Monty's Double
programme.desc: World War II drama. A low-ranking officer who resembles General Montgomery is recruited to play his double in an elaborate plan to trick the Nazis.

programme.title: GAA Live
programme.desc: Holly Hamilton presents live coverage of the All-Ireland Hurling final between Galway and Limerick from a sold-out Croke Park. It's one of the standout days in the Irish sporting calendar and is steeped in history, pride and fierce rivalry. Holly is joined in the studio by All-Ireland winners Paul Murphy and Séamus Flanagan, and Neil McManus. Mark Sidebottom provides pitch-side updates, with commentary by Thomas Niblock and Clare legend Jamesie O’Connor. Stars from the worlds of sport and entertainment are set to join the coverage for a special day in Irish sport.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the Flog It! team are in Greater Manchester, where over 800 people come along to have their items valued at Stockport Town Hall. Paul is joined by experts Mark Stacey and Philip Serrell as they pick out their favourite antiques to take to auction. Mark is wowed by a William De Morgan ceramic tile, Paul finds some etchings by artist John Buckland Wright and Philip discovers a gold necklace that surprises everyone at auction.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona and Philip unravel the mystery of a suspected Fabergé flower, kept hidden in a shoebox in Liverpool but believed to have belonged to royalty – and worth a small fortune!

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Antiques Roadshow comes from the beautiful grounds of the imposing Elizabethan mansion Wollaton Hall in Nottingham. Silver expert Kate Flitcroft is shaken, not stirred, by a stunning fire extinguisher cocktail shaker that turns up, while ceramics expert Eric Knowles meets his match with a cheeky toy monkey popping out of a musical pineapple. Fiona Bruce arrives in style on an iconic Chopper bike and tells the story of Raleigh Bicycles, founded in Nottingham in the 1880s. She learns about local activist George Powe, who campaigned to secure employment opportunities for black workers at Raleigh in the 1950s, and hears from two former employees about life inside what was once the world’s largest bicycle factory. Jewellery expert Susan Rumfitt challenges Fiona to guess the value of three intriguing cameo items, and collectables expert Hilary Kay is surprised to discover some of Queen Victoria’s personal undergarments with a sizeable price tag to boot.

programme.title: The Guernsey Literary and Potato Peel Pie Society
programme.desc: In London, 1946, author Juliet Ashton's career has taken off, but she is not enjoying promoting her detective stories to eager audiences on a book tour. When she receives a letter from a Guernsey pig farmer inviting her to attend a meeting of his literary society, she eagerly accepts. What Juliet discovers about her new friends and their secrets leads to her staying on the island much longer than she planned.

programme.title: Golf: The Open
programme.desc: Highlights from the final day of the 154th Open as the Champion Golfer of the Year is crowned at Royal Birkdale. Previous winners at the famous Southport links include Arnold Palmer, Tom Watson, Padraig Harrington and Jordan Spieth, who secured a dramatic victory when the Claret Jug was last won here back in 2017. World number one Scottie Scheffler started the week as defending champion following his emphatic triumph at Royal Portrush in 2025, with world number two Rory McIlroy hoping to bookend his major season with a Claret Jug to add to the Green Jacket he won at the Masters in April.

programme.title: ODI Cricket
programme.desc: Highlights from the third ODI of 2026 between England and India.

programme.title: Margrete: Queen of the North
programme.desc: Powerful and tense historical epic about the untold story of the woman who united Scandinavia in a time of war. The year is 1402. Margrete has achieved what no man has managed before: she has gathered Denmark, Norway and Sweden into a peace-oriented union that she rules through her adopted son, King Erik. But a conspiracy is in the making, and Margrete finds herself in an impossible dilemma that could shatter her life’s work.

programme.title: The Tech Billionaire Takeover
programme.desc: A new generation of tech billionaires believe their technologies can replace democracy as we know it and upend the global financial system. Matt Shea gets inside access to this new super-elite, visiting the sovereign nations they're building and tracing their growing political connections to the Trump administration. Are these men trying to take over the world?

programme.title: Ann Droid
programme.desc: Sue’s husband Dave died two years ago, and now her only son, Michael, is moving out – again – to try to fix his marriage – again. But the good news is he has got Sue a surprise to help her live independently: an Ann Droid Z58/100 humanoid care robot. Sue really does not want a robot in her home, especially one that causes chaos straightaway. But is this robot actually the companion she most needs?

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Maned wolves arrive at the park for the first time. Before they can meet their future capybara and tapir housemates, the keepers must test their reactions.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and Lauren help Emma downsize a home full of memories after losing her husband. Finding items full of stories in his home library, the family will turn his loss into legacy.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Charlotte fixes a little Range Rover that represents a big globetrotting adventure, Chris takes on a historic scrapbook, and Dean restores a trailblazing ballerina’s pointe shoes.

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: In our fields and hedgerows are plants that are delicious as well as plants that are deadly, as Rick learns from a professional forager who takes him on a tour around the beautiful Camel Estuary. Inspired, Rick creates his own version of a Greek Horta pie with foraged greens. Afterwards, it’s a trip down memory lane with his long-time fish supplier Matthew Stevens, who remembers Cornwall before the tourists came. Over a pint with actor, comedian and passionate Cornishman Ed Rowe, star of the BAFTA winning film Bait, Rick examines the impact of second home ownership on Cornish communities.

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle celebrate 25 years together on Animal Park, looking back at some of the animals, keepers and unforgettable moments they have shared, while new stories continue to unfold across the park. After years of planning, it's a landmark day as four hippos take their first steps beyond their new home and into Half Mile Pond. The keepers have spent months preparing for this moment, but once the hippos venture into the water, there's no telling what they'll do next, or whether they'll come back when called. As part of the anniversary celebrations, Ben and Kate revisit the story of Kadu, one of the park's most loved Amur tigers. When a routine procedure uncovered devastating news, her keepers faced one of the hardest decisions of their careers in a story that remains one of the most emotional moments in Animal Park's history. Elsewhere, the Farmyard celebrates the arrival of adorable pygmy goat kids, while Megan McCubbin meets a critically endangered cotton-top tamarin who has recently given birth to a precious new baby, giving hope for the future of the species.

programme.title: The Moment of Proof
programme.desc: When a missing woman is found buried in her own back garden, family and friends raise suspicions about her long-term boyfriend. But police don’t have much to go on – until he flees to Estonia. It’s only when some special CCTV comes to light that they have their moment of proof. Also, in Cheshire, police investigating a spate of burglaries have loads of CCTV of their man – they just don’t know who he is. That is before they get the DNA results from a head torch he’s accidentally dropped.

programme.title: Expert Witness
programme.desc: When a man kills his ex-wife, a forensic archaeologist helps determine if it was premeditated murder, and a cell site analyst follows a phone trail that convicts violent bank robbers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Programme in which experts uncover the various tricks of the property auction trade

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams go shopping for antiques in Exeter with experts Danny Sebastian and John Cameron. Natasha discovers more about fossilist Mary Anning.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp and his sidekick Sarah Greene host this quiz show where the contestants race on moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers stop them in their tracks. Five contestants battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: For Sharon and Andrew, farming and family is their whole life. With five grown-up children and several grandkids, there’s an army of willing helpers on hand to lead their Coloured Ryeland sheep into the ring at the Mid Devon Show. Luckily, show day is very much a family affair, so everyone is around to celebrate some impressive results. Farming couple Gillian and Ian are also competing with their Coloured Ryelands. They may be leading the way in rewilding their Devon farm, but Gillian is quite new to showing her sheep in competition. Along with pre-show nerves, there are tears when she discovers the judges' decision.

programme.title: The Hidden World of Hospitality with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: The changeable UK weather can bring sharp peaks and troughs in revenue for hospitality businesses. Tom heads to a remote hotel, an ice cream parlour and his own restaurant to look at inventive methods businesses employ to cope with fluctuating seasonal demand.

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: Three charismatic animal families, capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis, strive to raise their families against a backdrop of extraordinary landscapes and huge extremes of weather - a beautiful yet dangerous world full of jaguars and caimans. An intimate journey to the heart of a spectacular country, this first chapter of the trilogy sees the babies taking their first steps during a brief pause between seasonal extremes.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Milestones Museum in Basingstoke, which recreates the streets of Victorian and Edwardian Hampshire. Experts James Lewis and Nick Davies hope to find the best antiques and collectibles, and Paul discovers more about the industries and people that made Basingstoke a magnet during the Industrial age.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, four previous players who came close to winning but never quite made it - Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. With their second chance at winning a prize, will they opt for the House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, four previous players who came close to winning but never quite made it - Fern Britton, JB Gill, John Kearns and Sally Lindsay - take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. With their second chance at winning a prize, will they opt for the House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Global Eye
programme.desc: A weekly programme from the BBC World Service, bringing forensic journalism from all corners of the world by BBC Eye, the World Service's critically acclaimed investigative unit, and featuring expert global correspondents getting to the heart of the biggest international stories. This week, in the aftermath of a political earthquake in Hungary, Stephanie Hegarty explores whether the downfall of Viktor Orbán could be a warning sign for populist leaders everywhere, and BBC Eye investigates the Russian nationalists who are taking the law into their own hands.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: Clive Myrie presents another heat of the classic quiz where contenders take on the famous black chair under the glare of the spotlight and the ticking clock in an attempt to become the next Mastermind champion. The specialist subjects are British and Irish cheeses, the Roman general Sulla, the television series Jonathan Creek and the composer Johannes Brahms.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts the quiz where knowledge only gets you so far – the vital extra ingredients are logic and lateral thinking. A team keen on science fiction take on three fans of science fact, facing such questions as: what connects question hard, comically criticise, illegally hunt and pus-filled swelling?

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In another first-round heat, students from Durham take on defending champions Manchester. Amol Rajan asks the questions.

programme.title: Evolution
programme.desc: How did the ostrich come to lay the biggest egg on earth? Chris Packham explores the way reproduction has shaped life on earth and the surprising evolutionary origins of sex, eggs and birds. It's a four-billion-year journey contained within a shell.

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul and Bob are on the River Severn in Worcestershire to fish for zander. It’s a new species for both of them, and they enlist the help of a local guide, Tim Maslen to find the best spots. Paul explains to Bob how he has never even fished for zander before, let alone caught one. After learning a difficult new technique and staying in a converted lightship, they meet up with This Country star Charlie Cooper to talk about his passion for fishing.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night
programme.desc: Oscar-winning thriller about a bigoted sheriff in a small Mississippi cotton town who finds himself forced into working with a black homicide expert from Philadelphia.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The next stop on Anita's road trip is West Mersea in Essex. While the county may be known for engineering and car manufacturing, along its coastline one industry has a much longer history. Oysters have been harvested here for more than 2,000 years, cultivated in the creeks around Mersea Island. Over the decades, oyster farming has adapted to changing environmental conditions and the arrival of the non-native Pacific rock oyster. To experience what oyster harvesting is like today, Anita heads onto the brackish waters to meet the people farming oysters. She also meets the team investigating how the non-native Pacific rock oyster is affecting the local ecosystem and whether it could play a role in helping coastal habitats respond to rising sea levels. Along the way, Anita looks back through the Countryfile archives at the people and projects shaping life around our coastline.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts another five days of the quiz, with the action kicking off in Dundee. Traitors star Paul Gorton resumes his role as unreliable tour guide, mixing truths with tall tales as the celebrity players attempt to separate unbelievable facts from fiction. Comedian and presenter Susan Calman, internet star Max Fosh and DJ Noreen Khan are this week’s guests. A daily winner is declared at the end of each show, with scores carried forward throughout the week to decide the week's overall champion.

programme.title: Scamanda
programme.desc: As an investigative reporter looks closer at Amanda's story, the cracks begin to show. And while Amanda continues to blog and fundraise, suspicion spreads.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Northern Ireland’s County Down, Jules Hudson helps Siobhan, with support from her sister, search for the perfect Emerald Isle home for her and her husband after years of living in the West Midlands. With golf firmly on the agenda, properties include a stunning house on the shores of the iconic Strangford Lough. Jules also meets a barrister turned farmer and his wife, who swapped courtrooms for country life.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: After losing both parents, a pro-footballer’s daughter calls in JB and JJ to help turn heirlooms into cash for Una’s goal - a dream holiday with her beloved pooch, Patricia.

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The Great British Sewing Bee returns with brand new host Sophie Willan. Over ten weeks, 12 of the country’s best home sewers compete to be crowned winner of the Sewing Bee crown. In the opening week, judges Patrick Grant and Esme Young set three challenges on the theme of the British summer. First up, the Pattern Challenge sees them crafting summer shorts with a patch pocket. Next, in the 90-minute Transformation Challenge, barbecue aprons become bags. Finally, there is a Sewing Bee first in the Made-to-Measure challenge as the sewers are asked to create garments to fit themselves. They become their own models as they make outfits for their ideal summer’s day, and once complete, the catwalk is all theirs. One sewer will impress and earn Garment of the Week, but for another, it’s the end of the road as they leave the sewing room for good.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Kemah Bob, JJ Chalmers, Kevin Clifton and Victoria Derbyshire take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quick-fire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games decanter, or will they be tempted by the House of Games kitchen composter?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp hosts this quiz show with sidekick Sarah Greene where contestants race on moving podiums across the studio floor. Correct answers get the players moving, while wrong answers stop them in their tracks. Five contestants battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - make it to the finish line before your rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the £5,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: In this episode, the Beechgrove team marry sustainability with staying on-trend. Carole, Kirsty and Mairi are introduced to the plant craft of Kokedama by local house plant expert Fiona Macdonald. At her Edinburgh allotment, Kirsty demonstrates that you can still be on-trend and sustainable even if you are short of space, as she shows how to create a living-herb wall made from recycled materials.

programme.title: Cricket: The Hundred
programme.desc: Coverage of The Hundred 100-ball cricket tournament.

programme.title: Cricket: The Hundred
programme.desc: Coverage of The Hundred 100-ball cricket tournament.

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Nish Kumar, Mark Steel and Suzi Ruffell join Sandi Toksvig and Alan Davies for some thrill and spills.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The magnificent Jen Brister introduces comedians Esther Manito and Adam Rowe to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: The History Boys
programme.desc: Alan Bennett's adaptation of his acclaimed, long-running play set in 1980s Yorkshire. A class of likely lads, caught in a clash of educational styles as they prepare to apply to Oxford or Cambridge, find their loyalties as well as their intellects tested.

programme.title: Natalie Cassidy: Caring Together
programme.desc: Natalie discovers the power of caring volunteers, and how mind and body work together to help people recover and move forward.

programme.title: This is CBBC!
programme.desc: Tune every day from 7.00am.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Children's animation. Shaun comes across some newly hatched chicks who think he is their mum. He is determined to get rid of them, but how?

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: Danger Mouse and Penfold must travel to the Twystyverse to stop the evil tyrant Baron Silas von Penfold from taking over both versions of the Earth.

programme.title: Dennis & Gnasher Unleashed!
programme.desc: Eager to prove her babysitting ability for a permit, Gran’s on the hunt for a baby stand-in. Walter is suspiciously all too willing to offer up Bertie. Can the gang help Gran wrangle the reluctant baby-sitt-ee?

programme.title: Boy Girl Dog Cat Mouse Cheese
programme.desc: Mouse is tested on his experiment to create the perfect family and is sure he will fail! However, his siblings convince him to hire actors to play perfect versions of themselves.

programme.title: Newsround
programme.desc: Topical news magazine for children.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Plankton's pet amoeba Spot has puppies.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.desc: The Lemmings' magic keyring has the incredible power to miniaturise any object.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog have a new job washing cars. While Duck carefully cleans each car to a perfect shine, Frog dreams of driving a swanky sports car.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog are working at a woodland holiday park when they both get lost in the wilderness. Frog heroically leads the way - he's just not sure where to!

programme.title: Counterfeit Cat
programme.desc: Max struggles to deal with Gark’s relationship with Arugula, his invisible girlfriend.

programme.title: The Amazing World of Gumball
programme.desc: Gumball and Darwin accidentally destroy an overdue DVD rental.

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Wildlife superstar Robert Irwin puts his skills to the test with a photography challenge. We check out the latest read in the Blue Peter Book Club and find out how to make some tasty football biscuits!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: Freestyle footballer James takes on the biggest pros as he battles to keep his championship crown.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson FC
programme.desc: Jordan feels the pressure as the documentary cameras follow him and Jamie Johnson, and Madison takes Chelsea and Charlie on an amazing day out.

programme.title: Girl Troop vs Aliens
programme.desc: Tilda and Ginny race to Brighthawk Camp with aliens in pursuit, while Kiara rallies the troops for battle - can the Brighthawks defend their camp against aliens?

programme.title: The Lady Grace Mysteries
programme.desc: Grace has a new plan to discover the traitor at court using Masou’s new play. But she will need all her friends to play their part, whether they know it or not.

programme.title: Go Get Arty
programme.desc: It's a brothers v sisters basketball battle - who will build the ultimate court? Alex Horne draws an axolotl with a bottle, and the family remix a masterpiece at Tate Britain.

programme.title: Cooking Buddies
programme.desc: Buddy and Conker are joined in their cooking den by Alex, a figure skater who wants a meal that will warm him and his friend up after training on the ice! Alex likes meat, but his friend is vegetarian, so he needs Buddy’s help with something that will keep them both happy... Buddy knows that burgers are a winner as they’re super-flexible, so he creates the ultimate burger for Alex that’s half beef mince and half lentils that his friend can swap for veggie mince and lentils. Using lentils also means you use less meat, which is good for you and the planet – win, win! In return, Alex takes Buddy to train on the ice, teaching him the skills to skate forwards, backwards, stop and build up some speed – and how to style it out when you fall over! It turns out that the key to good form is making lemons with your feet – so there’s more in common with the kitchen than Buddy thought! Join Buddy, Conker and Alex as they glide through the recipe and ice rink in a super-tasty episode of Cooking Buddies! You can get all of Buddy’s recipes, and loads more, at bbc.co.uk/food.

programme.title: Operation Ouch!
programme.desc: Gross Alert! Exploring the weird and wonderful world of medicine. From sneezes and snot to pimples and poo, nothing escapes the Doctors' crazy experiments!

programme.title: Horrible Histories
programme.desc: Horrible Histories reveals history's most monstrous musicians. From the earliest screeches of the neanderthal, right through to Jimi Hendrix burning his guitar, Rattus Rattus guides us through the story of music making, even bumping into Simon Cowell at Beethoven's 'H Factor' audition. Starring Ryan Sampson and Gemma Whelan.

programme.title: Horrible Science
programme.desc: Greg Davies and Alex Horne host Scientific Taskmaster, and Einstein unpacks why belly flops hurt. Also, meet superhero team the Insect Revengers.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: SpongeBob visits Bubbletown.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Sandy invites Karen and Mrs Puff out for a night on the town.

programme.title: SpongeBob SquarePants
programme.desc: Sandy disturbs a day of jelly fishing.

programme.title: Submarine Jim
programme.desc: Jim captures a human to find out the ending of a movie.

programme.title: Submarine Jim
programme.desc: Jim tries to be Captain Nice, but the Urchins take advantage.

programme.title: Submarine Jim
programme.desc: Jim sets out to rescue sardines lost during a newspaper run.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: Duncan sees an opportunity for a break from daycare when an angry skunk hunts down Chef.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: When a substitute teacher fills in, Duncan thinks it's going to be the best day ever.

programme.title: Total Dramarama
programme.desc: Owen's newly found gloves make him great at everything, but this comes at a high price.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: When Duck accidentally gets a job as the band conductor at the local park, Frog is keen to play trumpet in the band - but Duck is even more keen to stop him!

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck and Frog must move a famous pop star’s possessions from one mansion to another. However, they are unable to do it with anything remaining in one piece.

programme.title: Duck and Frog
programme.desc: Duck is working overnight in a museum on his own. To keep him company, Duck makes a Frankenstein Frog, which soon runs rampage. It’s up to Frog to save the day!

programme.title: Malory Towers
programme.desc: Angry about not receiving a present from Paris, Celeste hides in the stables. However, she is trapped there with Matron when the stables catch fire.

programme.title: Jamie Johnson
programme.desc: Phoenix FC have a new home and a new coach. But can Jamie adapt to his new circumstances, prove himself as captain — and cope with life without his best friend Jack?

programme.title: The Next Step: Cheer
programme.desc: When a legendary coach abruptly leaves a highly successful cheerleading gym, the team are left in disarray, The Next Step’s Kenzie steps in to take over what’s left of the team, desperate to prove she is more than just a dancer, and that she can build this rag-tag group of athletes into a contender for nationals.

programme.title: Stage Stars
programme.desc: With Christmas around the corner, the juniors give a festive performance at the local town fair. Meanwhile, it's time for Encore's end-of-term performance.

programme.title: The Football Academy
programme.desc: Enter the fun world of a football-mad family, where life is never quiet, and meet para-footballer Ellie as she travels to the Women's Euros.

programme.title: Bro's in Control
programme.desc: It’s Bro’s Sports Day, and Adam and Joe break out the clubs for a mini golf challenge.

programme.title: The Dumping Ground
programme.desc: Growing up in a care home brings all sorts of tough challenges, but these kids know that if they stick together, they can get through anything life throws at them.

programme.title: Armorsaurs
programme.desc: Helmsworth demands answers from Switchblade.

programme.title: Shaun the Sheep
programme.desc: Shaun escapes through a ‘portal’ inside Shirley’s wool to flee an annoying Bitzer, only to emerge into a chaotic version of the farm without him. Desperate to escape, he repeatedly jumps back inside Shirley, emerging somewhere worse each time!

programme.title: Danger Mouse
programme.desc: In order to stop a deranged Victorian time-travelling chimp from uninventing the world, Danger Mouse and Penfold must arrive in the past just before the nick of time.

programme.title: Super Happy Magic Forest
programme.desc: It’s been ages since their last quest, and the heroes are losing their edge, so Mr Friendlyface invents a strange and terrible monster that he says is stomping through the forest causing havoc, and sends the heroes on a fake quest to get rid of it.
